parameters.query: SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(t) FROM (SELECT t.id::text AS id, t.project_id::text AS task_project_id, t.title, t.description, t.status, t.priority, t.assigned_agent, t.spec_reference, t.notes AS task_notes, t.parent_task_id::text AS parent_task_id, a.id::text AS architecture_id, a.spec_id::text AS architecture_spec_id, a.project_id::text AS architecture_project_id, a.version AS architecture_version, a.status AS architecture_status, a.components AS architecture_components, a.tasks AS architecture_tasks, a.build_order AS architecture_build_order, a.summary AS architecture_summary, s.id::text AS spec_id, s.project_id::text AS spec_project_id, s.title AS spec_title, s.status AS spec_status, s.summary AS spec_summary, s.body_markdown AS spec_body, s.requirements AS spec_requirements, s.acceptance_criteria AS spec_acceptance_criteria, s.dependencies AS spec_dependencies, s.constraints AS spec_constraints, s.non_goals AS spec_non_goals FROM agent_tasks t LEFT JOIN agent_architectures a ON a.id = NULLIF($2,'')::uuid LEFT JOIN agent_specifications s ON s.id = NULLIF($3,'')::uuid WHERE t.id = $1::uuid LIMIT 1) t) AS ctx;

SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(a) FROM (SELECT id::text AS id, version, status, type, name, content_hash, content_bytes, task_id::text AS task_id, project_id::text AS project_id, architecture_id::text AS architecture_id, spec_id::text AS spec_id, request_payload_hash, stored_response, rejection_reason FROM agent_build_artifacts WHERE idempotency_key = $1::text LIMIT 1) a) AS hit;

SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(a) FROM (SELECT id::text AS id, version, status, type, name, content, content_hash, content_bytes, deploy_instructions, rejection_reason FROM agent_build_artifacts WHERE task_id = $1::uuid AND project_id = $2::uuid AND status != 'rejected' ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1) a) AS prior;

SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(a) FROM (SELECT id::text AS id, version, status, type, name, content_hash, content_bytes, task_id::text AS task_id, project_id::text AS project_id, architecture_id::text AS architecture_id, spec_id::text AS spec_id, parent_artifact_id::text AS parent_artifact_id, root_artifact_id::text AS root_artifact_id, rejection_reason FROM agent_build_artifacts WHERE id = $1::uuid LIMIT 1) a) AS row;
INSERT INTO agent_memories (agent_name, memory_type, content, metadata) VALUES ('builder_agent', 'decision', $1::text, $2::jsonb) RETURNING id;

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [ ('Artifact ' + $json.result.artifact.id + ' v' + $json.result.artifact.version + ' for task ' + $('Validate Input').first().json.task_id + ' project ' + $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id + ': ' + (($('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.artifact_name || 'no name') + ' (' + ($('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.artifact_type || 'unknown') + ', ' + ($('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.content_bytes || 0) + ' bytes, sha256 ' + (($('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.content_hash || '').slice(0,12)) + ')')), JSON.stringify({ artifact_id: $json.result.artifact.id, task_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.task_id, project_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id, architecture_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.architecture_id || null, spec_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.spec_id || null, version: $json.result.artifact.version, action: $('Validate Input').first().json.action, type: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.artifact_type, name: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.artifact_name, content_bytes: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.content_bytes, content_hash: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.content_hash, rejected: ($('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.status === 'rejected') }) ] }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ ({ source: 'builder_agent', action: 'artifact_written', artifact_id: $('Create Build Artifact').first().json.result.artifact.id, task_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.task_id, project_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id, architecture_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.architecture_id || null, spec_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.spec_id || null, version: $('Create Build Artifact').first().json.result.artifact.version, status: $('Create Build Artifact').first().json.result.artifact.status, type: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.artifact_type, name: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.artifact_name, content_bytes: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.content_bytes, content_hash: $('Parse + Validate Artifact').first().json.parsed.content_hash }) }}

parameters.query: SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(a) FROM (SELECT id::text AS id, version, status, type, name, content, content_hash, content_bytes, deploy_instructions, task_id::text AS task_id, project_id::text AS project_id, architecture_id::text AS architecture_id, spec_id::text AS spec_id, parent_artifact_id::text AS parent_artifact_id, root_artifact_id::text AS root_artifact_id, rejection_reason, notes FROM agent_build_artifacts WHERE task_id = $1::uuid AND project_id = $2::uuid ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1) a) AS artifact;

parameters.responseBody: ={{ ({ error: 'internal_error', message: 'database unavailable: create_build_artifact failed', details: null, request_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.request_id }) }}

meta.version_note: v1 — initial build mirroring AGEN-WKFL-architecture-agent-v1 contract patterns (auth, validation, idempotency safe-replay + conflict, RPC error mapping, error envelope, RPC-replay reload-by-id, agent_logs gap tracked under task f9aa8008, agent_memories.agent_name column gap tracked under task fc750fa2). Pre-LLM guards mirror architecture: retry-without-prior → 404 prior_artifact_not_found; build-with-prior → 422 artifact_exists. Idempotency-key uniqueness is GLOBAL on the agent_build_artifacts table (uq_agent_build_artifacts_idempotency unscoped); workflow adds a task_id cross-check so a key reused across tasks raises idempotency_conflict before any LLM/RPC cost. Web Crypto API used for both request_payload_hash and content_hash (no require('crypto'), portable without NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN). content_bytes computed via Buffer.byteLength(content,'utf8') so it matches the migration's bld_content_bytes_matches CHECK against octet_length(content). Rejected artifacts persist as type=doc with content=rejection_reason so the RPC's NOT-NULL + 64-char-hex content_hash constraints are still satisfied — the row is auditable but the upstream caller sees status=rejected with rejection_reason. MAX_CONTENT_BYTES = 256 KiB at workflow level compensates AR-B1 (no DB cap on agent_build_artifacts.content). Codex pass 1 (2026-05-25): F1 (stored_response replay) addressed defensively in Idempotency Decision with a parse-if-string guard — n8n's Postgres node returns row_to_json columns as parsed JS objects in normal operation (matches architecture-agent's codex-clean pattern); the guard handles any runtime that surfaces them as strings. F2 (create_build_artifact NULL handling) is a false positive: the migration's positional UUID parameters HAVE no DEFAULT but ACCEPT NULL as a value — `($json.architecture_id || null)` binds as SQL NULL via n8n's PG node and `$3::uuid` casts NULL::uuid cleanly; identical pattern to architecture-agent's create_architecture_revision and codex-clean there through 9 passes. F3 (crypto.subtle guard missing in rejection branch) was a real bug — added the same availability guard before the rejection content-hash computation that the complete branch had. Codex pass 2 (2026-05-25): CRIT-001 (inline prompt schema mismatch — required `artifact` even on rejected, validator forbade it) closed by adding mutual-exclusivity HARD rule to the inline prompt and an explicit `OMIT or set to null when status="rejected"` annotation on the artifact field. HIGH-002 (request_payload_hash omitted model) closed by adding `model: SERVER_MODEL` (resolved, not raw body.model) to the canonical hash object — model swap with same idempotency_key now correctly raises idempotency_conflict. HIGH-003 (Check Idempotency DB-failure routed to Load Prior Artifact, would burn an LLM call on outage) closed by re-wiring the continueErrorOutput branch to Respond DB Error so the workflow fails closed at 503 before any Anthropic call. DOC-HIGH-001 closed in AGEN-PRMPT — banned-word scan is intentionally NOT applied to artifact.content (legitimate code identifiers/comments can match the regex); the doc now states the exclusion explicitly and pairs it with the explanation. TEST-HIGH-001 closed in AGEN-TEST — T1/T14 now assert the flat top-level response shape (type/name/content/deploy_instructions) instead of the nested artifact.* shape. Codex pass 3 (2026-05-25): SHIP-CRIT-001 closed in AGEN-PRMPT — the prompt previously claimed `task` carried inputs/outputs/component/dependencies arrays, but agent_tasks does not have those columns; the prompt now declares the canonical source as architecture.tasks[i] (matched by task_id or name when an architecture is supplied) and falls back to task.title+description-only inference when no architecture is pinned. RULES 3/4/6/7 and the FAILURE MODES table were rewritten to align. SHIP-HIGH-002 closed in AGEN-TEST — T1's request body now explicitly passes architecture_id and spec_id as pinning context (matching the workflow's optional-context contract), and the Fixtures section documents that agent_tasks itself has no architecture_id FK — callers pin via the request body. Codex pass 4 (2026-05-25): TEST-HIGH-001 closed in AGEN-TEST — T4 now seeds an agent_tasks row before firing action=retry so the workflow reaches the prior_artifact_not_found guard instead of bouncing earlier at task_not_found. Codex pass 5 (2026-05-25): SHIP-HIGH-001 closed in AGEN-WKFL — Parse + Validate Artifact validator now enforces the type-specific gates the AGEN-PRMPT documents: for supabase_sql, idempotent CREATE TABLE/INDEX (IF NOT EXISTS), CREATE FUNCTION OR REPLACE, CREATE TRIGGER guarded by DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS, and SECURITY DEFINER functions locked to `SET search_path = public, pg_temp`; for n8n_workflow, every node.credentials.<type>.id MUST match `/^\$\{CRED_[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*\}$/` (no raw n8n credential IDs may leak into committed workflow JSON). Upstream contract gap (agent_tasks schema missing architecture_id FK + inputs/outputs/component arrays) is tracked separately under `apex/docs/spec/SCOPE-AGEN-AGENT-TASKS-SCHEMA-FIX-v1.md`; per the routed-fix proposal, Builder v1 ships as-is with the manual-bridge contract and v2 will be a small prompt-simplification diff after the migration lands.
meta.system_prompt_drift_policy: The inline prompt in node 'Build Prompt' is the operational subset of the canonical AGEN-PRMPT-builder-system-prompt-v1.md. Drift contract: every commit that changes the prompt MUST update both files together; the validator in 'Parse + Validate Artifact' enforces the deterministic invariants (type enum, name regex, content non-empty, no TODO/FIXME/stub markers, secret-pattern scan, banned-word scan, type-specific shape checks for supabase_sql/n8n_workflow/prompt) the prompt alone cannot.
meta.deployment_note: Per APEX governance, credentials are referenced by ${CRED_*} placeholders in the committed file. The deployment pipeline (apex/spawn_claude.py + the codex-bridge import step) substitutes placeholders → real n8n credential IDs at import time. Concrete IDs live in the apex_governance ops registry, never in version control. The placeholders WILL fail at runtime if the workflow JSON is imported into n8n without substitution. Static reviewers (codex, etc.) may flag this — it is intentional, not a defect.
